preview_text:
Spain enters as the model's heavy favourite at 71%, leaving Uruguay just 8% to claim victory on a neutral ground. The gap reflects a substantial asymmetry in the forecast; Spain's win probability is nearly nine times larger than Uruguay's.

The draw sits at 21%, suggesting the match carries meaningful competitive texture despite Spain's dominance. A one-goal margin remains plausible; the spread between a Spanish win and a stalemate is only about half the gap to an Uruguay upset, implying Spain will face real pressure to convert chances rather than run away with proceedings.

The model and sportsbooks diverge notably on the extremes. Bookmakers assign Uruguay roughly double the win probability the model does—a gap of 8 percentage points—while rating Spain's chances 9 points lower. Draw probabilities align closely. Polymarket prices track the book consensus more closely than the model forecast, particularly for Uruguay. The dashboard presents both views; which better reflects true match likelihood remains undetermined.
